title: Memory Function (Memoization) Process for the Knapsack Problem
description: A diagram showing how a recursive algorithm with memoization avoids redundant calculations by checking a results table before proceeding with a computation.
relationships: Function call first checks base cases
If not a base case, function checks the memoization table for an existing result
If a result exists in the table, it is returned immediately
If no result exists, the function computes the result by making recursive calls
The newly computed result is stored in the table before being returned
